LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ROBERTO BARRAGAN'S MOTION TO VACATE ORDER COMPELLING HIS CLAIMS TO ARBITRATION AND LIFT STAY AND AWARD SANCTIONS IS DENIED. [SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS.] FOR SIMILAR REASONS, PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS FOR DEFENDANTS' LATE PAYMENTS TO JAMS ARE DENIED. THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF THE ARBITRATION. (CODE CIV. PROC. SEC. 1281.4.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order Compelling His Claims To Arbitration And Lift Stay And Award Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROBERTO BARRAGAN'S Motion To Vacate Order Compelling His Claims To Arbitration And Lift Stay And Award Sanctions.

Plaintiff Roberto Barragan's "motion to vacate order compelling his claims to arbitration and lift stay and award sanctions" is denied. On May 7, 2021, this Court granted Defendants' motion to compel arbitration. JAMS issued a Commencement Letter to the parties on July 29, and the parties attended a status conference on November 17. Plaintiff now wishes to vacate the Court's order compelling arbitration pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure sections 1281.97 and 1281.98 because Defendants made two late payments. For the reasons set forth below, this request is denied, and the Court need not determine the meaning of "fees" and "costs" under sections 1281.97 and 1281.98.

The Court has no power to intervene on behalf of parties "stalled in arbitration," and it is "up to the arbitrator, and not the court, to grant relief for delay in bringing an arbitration to a resolution." (Titan/Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 487-488; see also JAMS Rule 11 ["Once appointed, the Arbitrator shall resolve disputes about the interpretation and applicability of these Rules."].) "An arbitration has a life of its own outside the judicial system. The trial court may not step into a case submitted to arbitration and tell the arbitrator what to do and when to do it: it may not resolve procedural questions, order discovery, determine the status of claims before the arbitrator or set the case for trial because of a party's alleged dilatory conduct." (Titan/Value, 29 Cal.App.4th at 489; see also Brock v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1796 ["Once a court grants the petition to compel arbitration and stays the action at law, the action at law sits in the twilight zone of abatement with the trial court retaining merely a vestigial jurisdiction over matters submitted to arbitration."].)

JAMS Rule 5 provides, "[t]he Arbitration is deemed commenced when JAMS issues a Commencement Letter" and "[t]he issuance of the Commencement Letter confirms that requirements for commencement have been met [and] that JAMS has received all payments required under the applicable fee schedule." Here, JAMS issued the Commencement Letter to the parties on July 29, 2021, indicating that Defendants paid all fees and costs required to initiate the arbitration proceeding.

JAMS Rule 6(c) provides, "[i]f, at any time, any Party has failed to pay fees or expenses in full, JAMS may order the suspension or termination of the proceedings." Here, neither party alleges that JAMS has ordered the suspension or termination of the arbitration proceeding.

JAMS Rule 13 provides, "[n]o Party may terminate or withdraw from an Arbitration after the issuance of the Commencement Letter (see Rule 5), except by written agreement of all Parties to the Arbitration." Here, Defendants have not agreed to terminate this arbitration.

Therefore, Plaintiff's motion to vacate the order compelling arbitration is denied. For similar reasons, Plaintiff's requests for sanctions for Defendants' late payments to JAMS are denied. The action is stayed pending the completion of the arbitration. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1281.4.)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
